rationale: Both catch the trust_remote_code default flip (matches the author's own flagged concern), but Y also explicitly surfaces and confirms the PR's central reviewer concern — that endpoint restrictions must resolve from the post-deploy-override pipeline, not the auto-detected one — and ties it to the added regression test, which X's sweep notes touch only implicitly without identifying it as the key fixed bug. Y's other findings (functools.cache never invalidating outside the one test file, UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES skipped in the else branch) are concretely grounded in the visible diff, whereas several of X's findings (frozenset/set mismatch, docstring staleness, missing field comment) reference files not shown in the given diff and read as speculative nitpicks. Both lack a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
